The survey is for the reference period April – June 2026 (Q1:2026-27). The Reserve Bank has been conducting the Order Books, Inventories and Capacity Utilisation Survey (OBICUS) of the manufacturing sector on a quarterly basis since 2008. items.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has appointed Shri Ravi Shankar as Executive Director (ED) with effect from July 1, 2026. Prior to his promotion as ED, Shri Shankar was serving as Adviser-In-Charge of the Department of Statistics and Information Management of the Bank. As a career central banker statistician, Shri Shankar has experience of over three decades in the Reserve Bank having worked in the areas of corporate, banking statistics, government securities market and settlement systems, debt management, and surveys in the Reserve Bank. items.title: FIFA World Cup powers shift to bigger TVs amid cautious consumer spending
items.summary: Despite a dip in India's smart TV market early in 2026, the FIFA World Cup is driving sales of premium, large-screen televisions. Consumers are increasingly opting for bigger displays and advanced technologies like QLED and MiniLED, with events like the World Cup acting as a significant upgrade trigger. Easy financing also aids this premiumisation trend, even as overall demand remains cautious.

items.title: Free trade agreements to give major boost to toy exports: Industry
items.summary: India's toy exports are poised for a surge following the finalisation of free trade agreements with the UK and the European Union, offering zero-duty access. Industry officials anticipate these pacts will unlock substantial growth, with the UK and EU markets currently representing 16% of India's USD 0.35 billion toy exports.

items.title: IT sector leads Dalal Street's winning streak; crude slump, Japan deals lift sentiment
items.summary: The Nifty 50 closed at 24,175.70, up 0.71 per cent, while the Sensex gained 0.75 per cent to settle at 77,502.

items.title: Sweltering heat sets summer-goods sales on fire; consumer brands hitch qcomm ride to big business
items.summary: India's scorching summer has fueled a surge in consumer goods sales, with quick-commerce platforms becoming the star performers. Ice creams, cold drinks and skincare products saw massive year-on-year growth as shoppers embraced instant delivery for seasonal needs.

items.title: India not exporting fuel directly to Russia, any supplies are trader-led: Puri
items.summary: India's Oil Minister, Hardeep Singh Puri, has stated that Indian companies are not directly supplying refined fuels to Russia. While reports suggested Moscow was importing gasoline from India to address shortages, Puri clarified that any Indian-origin refined fuel reaching Russia is likely through intermediaries. This distinction aims to address concerns about direct sales to Moscow.

items.title: From data infra to deep tech: Technology themes investors cannot ignore
items.summary: As India’s tech landscape rapidly evolves, new opportunities arise. Discover how gig economy platforms and their challengers are reshaping investment priorities, from operational efficiencies to the burgeoning needs of a massive gig workforce.

items.title: Brookfield wants to build AI data centers in London’s answer to Wall Street
items.summary: Brookfield's CEO said the firm is bringing data centers to London's Canary Wharf, as demand for AI infrastructure in the U.K. soars.

items.title: How Jewar Airport could trigger a Gurugram-like boom in Faridabad
items.summary: Faridabad, once a quiet NCR real estate market, is poised for rapid transformation driven by the upcoming Noida International Airport. Improved connectivity, particularly the proposed Faridabad-Jewar Expressway, is expected to mirror Gurugram's early airport-led growth. This, combined with existing infrastructure upgrades and attractive pricing, is drawing significant interest from homebuyers and investors, signaling a new era of development for the city.

items.title: Indian public relations industry pegged to reach Rs 4,500 cr by 2030: Report
items.summary: India's public relations sector is poised for significant growth, projected to hit Rs 4,500 crore by 2030. While the overall growth rate is moderating, indicating industry maturity, key sectors like startups, education, government, and FMCG are showing robust gains. Notably, government and startup client shares have surged dramatically, reshaping the industry's landscape. AI investment is also on the rise, though strategies vary across firms.

items.title: Expert view: Broader market to remain range-bound in medium term, says SKG Investment's Director
items.summary: Expert view: Kush Gupta, Fund Manager at SKG Investment, predicts a cautious recovery for the Indian stock market in 2026, hinging on crucial macroeconomic factors. Gupta emphasises opportunities in defence, financial services, and pharma.

items.title: Bank of Baroda settles NMC Health dispute for $600 million; Q1 credit grows 17%
items.summary: Bank of Baroda has settled a significant legal dispute with NMC Health entities for USD 600 million, resolving claims under UAE and UK regulations. The bank's Abu Dhabi branch facilitated the payment, concluding proceedings without admitting liability. In other news, BoB reported robust financial performance, with credit growth reaching 17% and total deposits rising 14% in the first quarter of FY27.

items.summary: In a post on X, Kamath pointed to the recent performance of the IT sector to explain why diversified investing often works better over the long term.

items.title: 28 stations, 35-km: Gurgaon's proposed metro corridor connects Sector 56 to Pachgaon with a Global City stop and Namo Bharat interchanges
items.summary: Gurgaon new metro corridor: Haryana Mass Rapid Transport Corporation has finalised the detailed project report for a 35-kilometre, 28-station metro corridor in Gurgaon, estimated at Rs 10,428 crore. The corridor will run from Sector 56 on Golf Course Extension Road to Pachgaon and will connect with the Rapid Metro and two proposed Namo Bharat lines.

items.title: Tank maker KNDS postpones IPO amid market struggles for defense
items.summary: Shareholders of the Amsterdam-based company have decided the process to IPO will proceed "upon the return of more favorable market conditions," KNDS said.

items.title: NRIs looking to park your dollars? Ujjivan SFB offers 7.5%, DBS up to 5.6% on FCNR deposits
items.summary: Banks are raising returns on FCNR(B) deposits after the RBI eased norms to attract foreign currency inflows. Ujjivan Small Finance Bank now offers 7.5% on 3–5-year USD FCNR deposits, while DBS Bank India provides up to 5.6%, giving NRIs more attractive options to park their dollar savings.

items.title: Merchant, arbitrage flows extend rupee's losing streak even as dollar slumps
items.summary: The Indian rupee weakened for the fourth straight session against the dollar on Thursday, despite a weaker dollar and likely central bank intervention. Market flows for arbitrage and merchant payments pressured the currency, which retreated from an early peak. Traders noted increased dollar demand and triggered stop-losses on rupee bets.

items.title: PM Modi, Japan PM Takaichi jointly inaugurate Maruti Suzuki's Kharkhoda plant
items.summary: Prime Minister Modi and his Japanese counterpart inaugurated Maruti Suzuki's expansive new manufacturing facility in Haryana, a significant investment bolstering India's automotive sector. This advanced plant, built with Industry 5.0 technologies and a focus on sustainability, marks a key step in the 'Make in India, Make for the World' initiative. The inauguration occurred during the India-Japan Economic Forum, highlighting deepening bilateral cooperation.

items.title: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
items.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

items.title: Beyond traditional advertising: Make your brand reach business decision makers
items.summary: Reaching the right audience today requires more than a successful campaign. As brands look to engage decision-makers in meaningful ways, industry platforms, credible storytelling and trusted business environments are becoming increasingly important.

items.title: Citi becomes No. 1 investment banker in India, earns $60 million in last 6 months: LSEG
items.summary: The jump gives Citi a 9.8% wallet share of India's total investment banking fee pool, which itself shrank 20% year-on-year to $614.1 million in the first half.

items.summary: Dalal Street saw gains for the second consecutive day, driven by technology stocks and easing crude oil prices. Nifty 50 rose 0.71% to 24,175, while the Sensex gained 0.75% to 77,502. The Nifty IT index surged 4.5%, marking its biggest gain since January.

items.title: Sensex jumps 600 points, Nifty 50 ends above 24,150; investors earn ₹3 lakh crore. What drove the market higher?
items.summary: The Sensex ended at 77,502.12, rising 579 points, or 0.75%, while the Nifty 50 rose by 170 points, or 0.71%, to end at 24,175.70. The Nifty Midcap 100 index rose by 0.48%, while the Smallcap 100 index jumped 1.25%.

items.title: Nifty eyes 24,500-24,800 in July as crude cools and FII selling slows; buy the dip towards 23,600–23,800: Dharmesh Shah
items.summary: Indian equity markets are poised for further gains, with Nifty expected to target 24,500-24,800 by July. ICICI Direct's Dharmesh Shah advises investors to "buy the dip" as key support at 23,800 holds firm. Favorable macro factors like easing crude oil prices and bond yields, alongside receding FII selling, are bolstering the market's upward trajectory. Banking stocks are also showing technical strength, potentially driving the broader index higher.

items.title: Medical device maker Zimmer Biomet to hire 500 employees in 3 years for tech centre in India
items.summary: Medical device giant Zimmer Biomet is set to significantly expand its Indian footprint by hiring 500 professionals over three years for its new Bengaluru technology hub. Focusing on AI, software engineering, and R&D, the company aims to drive innovation in orthopedic implants and surgical devices. This move underscores India's growing importance as a global capability center for healthcare firms.

items.summary: A Google spokesperson said the judgement "fails to recognise" the firm's "significant investment to ensure Android remains open."

items.title: Shantha Biologics to make injectable drug cartridges for Novo Nordisk
items.summary: Shantha Biologics has secured a significant contract with Danish pharmaceutical giant Novo Nordisk to manufacture cartridges for injectable medicines at its Hyderabad facility. This partnership highlights India's growing role in advanced pharmaceutical manufacturing, particularly for high-demand diabetes and obesity therapies. Shantha's FDA-approved, automated fill-finish lines will produce these critical components, underscoring the company's expanding capabilities in contract manufacturing for biologics.

items.title: Cracks in cola kingdom: India's duopoly faces biggest test in decades
items.summary: India's cola market is undergoing a significant transformation, moving beyond the traditional Coca-Cola vs. PepsiCo rivalry. Reliance's Campa revival ignited a price war, while ITC's new premium, sugar-free coconut cola signals a shift towards health, premiumization, and niche offerings. This evolving landscape caters to health-conscious consumers seeking alternatives, indicating a more dynamic and diversified future for the Indian beverage sector.

items.title: Coal India's SECL strengthens monsoon measures for uninterrupted supply
items.summary: SECL, a Coal India subsidiary, has implemented robust monsoon preparedness to ensure uninterrupted coal supplies crucial for India's energy security. The company achieved its best-ever first-quarter performance in production and offtake, with a significant portion directed to power utilities. SECL's extensive operations across Chhattisgarh and Madhya Pradesh underscore its vital role in meeting national energy demands.

items.title: NBCC awards Rs 955 crore contracts in Q1; signs MoU for AP Bhawan project
items.summary: NBCC has secured new construction contracts totaling Rs 955.13 crore in the first quarter of FY27. Key projects include the Central University of Kashmir's campus and an integrated township in West Bengal. Additionally, the company will build a new Andhra Pradesh Bhawan in New Delhi, marking significant project wins for the state-owned enterprise.

items.title: India's June palm oil imports fall to 14-month low as demand softens, dealers say
items.summary: India's palm oil imports plunged to a 14-month low in June, driven by weak consumer demand and a shrinking price advantage over rival oils. This significant drop, alongside reduced soyoil and sunflower oil purchases, signals a broader decline in edible oil imports. Factors like cautious buying by refiners, cooking gas issues, and intense heat are impacting consumption, potentially affecting global supply dynamics.

items.title: Oil prices on pace for fourth straight weekly loss as Trump sees progress in U.S.-Iran talks
items.summary: Oil prices fell more than 1% on Wednesday after talks between Washington and Tehran concluded.

items.title: Blinkit to remain undisputed market leader despite Amazon, Flipkart's QC entry, says Anand Rathi, sees 43% upside
items.summary: Despite concerns over Amazon and Flipkart intensifying competition in India's quick commerce market, Anand Rathi believes Blinkit remains the clear market leader due to its scale, strong customer retention and limited reliance on heavy discounts. The brokerage has reiterated its 'Buy' rating on Eternal with a target price of Rs 400.

items.title: Tata Technologies shares jump 9% after expanding strategic partnership with Tenneco
items.summary: Tata Technologies shares rose 9% to ₹729.20 after announcing an expanded partnership with Tenneco LLC. The agreement involves over $100 million investment to enhance engineering innovation and digital transformation, leveraging India's engineering talent for global automotive needs.

Analysts remain constructive, citing strong technical support, a record order book, expanding defence and export businesses, and healthy earnings growth prospects, suggesting the long-term investment case remains intact.

items.title: Swara Baby files DRHP with Sebi for Rs 1,000 crore IPO

The hygiene products manufacturer plans to use proceeds for capacity expansion, debt repayment, and acquisitions as it taps into India’s fast-growing disposable hygiene market.

items.title: Tata Technologies expands partnership with Tenneco in $100 million deal
items.summary: Tata Technologies and Tenneco LLC have inked a significant five-year deal exceeding $100 million to boost engineering and digital transformation. This expanded partnership, leveraging India's engineering talent, aims to accelerate product development and deploy AI-driven solutions across Tenneco's global automotive operations. The collaboration will enhance Tenneco's responsiveness to market shifts and strengthen its engineering prowess.

items.title: IBM to set up one of India's first quantum computers in Amaravati by September 2026
items.summary: Amaravati is set to become a major hub for quantum computing in India, with IBM announcing plans to install one of its advanced quantum systems there by September 2026. This move, driven by India's strong talent pool, aims to unlock significant commercial advantages across various sectors. The initiative aligns with Andhra Pradesh's vision to establish itself as a leading quantum technology center, fostering research and innovation.

items.title: Palantir’s Alex Karp says AI industry has ‘gone completely wrong’ with tokens; slams OpenAI, Anthropic
items.summary: Alex Karp, CEO of Palantir, criticized AI firms for relying on costly token models. He emphasized the need for companies to focus on return-on-investment approaches and raised concerns about data ownership and the rapid advancement of Chinese AI capabilities.

items.title: India's hidden semiconductor advantage: What's next in the chip journey?
items.summary: As India accelerates its semiconductor ambitions with a proposed second-phase incentive programme, Equirus says the country is well positioned to evolve from a global chip design hub into a full-stack semiconductor ecosystem. However, it cautions that execution challenges remain and maintains a cautious stance on select semiconductor-related stocks.

items.title: IT surge powers Nifty past 24,100; crude below $68 lifts sentiment
items.summary: IT giants led the Nifty, with Infosys rising 5.61% to ₹1,040.60 on over 1.15 crore shares worth ₹1,194.96 crores

items.title: Moody’s report suggests India’s private credit market could double by FY30
items.summary: India's private credit market is set for robust expansion, projected to reach $50 billion by FY30. Driven by escalating corporate funding needs and limitations faced by traditional banks, this alternative capital source is becoming a preferred option for businesses. Real estate, infrastructure, and promoter financing are key growth areas, attracting diverse investors seeking attractive returns for managing significant risks.

items.title: Bitcoin trades near $60,000, logs weakest month since June 2022 amid ETF outflows
items.summary: Bitcoin traded near the $60,000 level after recording its weakest monthly performance since June 2022, weighed down by heavy ETF outflows. Despite a modest rebound alongside Ethereum and major altcoins, analysts say macroeconomic factors, institutional demand and Federal Reserve policy will continue to shape cryptocurrency prices.

items.title: SBI Mutual Fund planning IPO launch on July 13, to raise $1.2 billion
items.summary: SBI Mutual Fund is set to launch its IPO on July 13, with a likely $1.2 billion issue size, making it one of the biggest in India’s financial sector. The offer-for-sale issue will see SBI and Amundi pare their stakes, with the price band announcement expected on July 9.

items.summary: The proposed 2 mtpa plant in Odisha would also help India reduce its reliance on imported aluminium, a light-weight metal that is increasingly used in electric vehicles

items.summary: Indian multiplexes witnessed a significant 21% year-on-year surge in box office collections during the first half of 2026. This robust growth is being attributed to a diverse array of successful films across languages and genres. Hit movies like 'Border 2' and 'Welcome to the Jungle' drew audiences back, signaling strong audience confidence in cinemas.

items.summary: Several midcap stocks emerged as standout performers, rewarding investors with impressive returns.

While domestic retail investors stepped in with record purchases, the Nikkei retreated as the once-red-hot AI-driven rally began losing momentum.

items.title: Ratnadeep Retail files DRHP with SEBI for Rs 400 crore IPO; promoters to sell 1.49 crore shares via OFS

The retailer plans to use the proceeds for debt repayment, store expansion and general corporate purposes as it strengthens its presence across southern India.

items.title: Macro headwinds are behind us; largecaps poised to outperform: Prashant Jain
items.summary: India's equity markets are poised for a positive turn as economic challenges recede, according to Prashant Jain. He highlights strong domestic fundamentals, improving external balances, and reasonable valuations, particularly favoring large-cap banking and IT stocks. Jain sees value in the IT sector despite near-term pressures and believes large private banks offer compelling opportunities as foreign selling eases, suggesting largecaps are set to outperform.

items.title: Flipkart names Vinay Vaidya as senior VP to bolster tech leadership
items.summary: Flipkart has appointed Vinay Vaidya as Senior Vice President of Technology for its supply chain, aiming to bolster its tech leadership and advance AI and platform capabilities. Vaidya, with extensive experience from Amazon and Tata Digital, will oversee technology across fulfilment, seller experience, and marketplace operations.

items.summary: India's inflation target is unlikely to be raised, with Reserve Bank of India Governor Sanjay Malhotra suggesting a potential long-term reduction. He highlighted the success of the current inflation-targeting framework in lowering average inflation. Despite expectations of a rise, inflation remains below the central bank's 4% target, and the economy is projected for continued robust growth.

items.summary: The startup already runs two brands - Emoha, an app-based at-home care service for seniors, and Epoch, which provides assisted living and specialized eldercare services, including dementia care, palliative care, recovery and rehabilitation, and long-term care.

items.title: New KPI on the block: Indian companies from banking to consulting start appraising leaders on AI implementation
items.summary: India Inc is stitching AI adoption into senior leadership performance appraisals. Companies like Axis Bank now demand measurable AI outcomes, signaling a shift from experimentation to accountability-driven AI integration.

items.title: All eyes on OMCs' petrol, diesel prices as Nayara cuts fuel rates
items.summary: OMCs raised petrol and diesel prices for the first time on 15 May, after holding on during the first two months of the war. Over four separate days, petrol and diesel prices were cumulatively raised by around ₹7.5 per litre.

items.title: 5kg LPG cylinder demand from immigrant labourers plummets up to 80% amid price surge

items.title: Growth engine revving as GST, auto sales rise despite global roadblocks
items.summary: India's economy shows robust growth in Q1 FY27, with GST collections soaring 13.9% to Rs 1.95 lakh crore in June. Car sales surged 24%, and power consumption rose 11.6%. Despite a dip in manufacturing PMI, strong import revenues and UPI transactions highlight economic resilience. Experts anticipate continued momentum, with potential for monthly GST collections to reach Rs 2 lakh crore.

The country also cemented its position as a top-tier hub for millionaires: it now has as many of them as Switzerland.

items.summary: The government has boosted rural employment wages by over 10% with the new Viksit Bharat-Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission-Gramin, replacing MGNREGA from July 1. Daily wages now start at ₹300, with an average of ₹327.4, and the annual employment guarantee is extended to 125 days. States with historically lower rates will see significant increases, though some critics deem the hike insufficient.

items.title: ICC chief calls for digitisation of trade papers for ease of access

He highlighted that 80-90% of trade paperwork remains paper-based, hindering interoperability and increasing costs. The ICC's Digital Standards Initiative has already digitized 21 key documents, with more underway, aiming to boost trade relevance for emerging economies and smaller businesses.

This surge in demand is now helping to stabilize Indian corn prices, with further growth anticipated from October.

items.title: EPF Scheme 2026: Your provident fund is getting simpler, smarter and more digital
items.summary: India's provident fund system has undergone a significant overhaul with the introduction of the Employees' Provident Fund Scheme, 2026, replacing the 1952 version. This modernization prioritizes digital compliance and simplified processes, aligning with new labor codes. Key changes include easier partial withdrawals for members and enhanced governance for employers, aiming for wider social security coverage and improved benefit delivery.

items.summary: India's economic outlook has improved as falling crude oil prices ease inflation, strengthen the balance of payments and prompt economists to raise GDP growth forecasts.
